content: Which of the following can react with $$\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$$ ?
explanation:
Among the given options, oxidation state of

sulphur in $$\mathrm{SO}_3^{-2}=+4$$

carbon in $$\mathrm{CO}_3^{-2}=+4$$

sulphur in $$\mathrm{SO}_4^{-2}=+6$$

nitrogen in $$\mathrm{NO}_3^{-}=+5$$

Since, $$\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$$ is a strong oxidising agent it can oxidise the species, which is not in its most possible positive oxidising state.

Thus, $$\mathrm{SO}_3^{-2}$$ can react with $$\mathrm{K}_2 \mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7$$ as follows:

$$\mathrm{Cr}_2 \mathrm{O}_7^{2-}(a q)+3 \mathrm{SO}_3^{2-}(a q)+8 \mathrm{H}^{+}(a q) \longrightarrow 2 \mathrm{Cr}^{3+}(a q)+3 \mathrm{SO}_4^{2-}(a q)+4 \mathrm{H}_2 \mathrm{O}(l)$$
